rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM R. LARSON ET AL VS. ROBERT M. LARSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 14, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT ROBERT LARSON, BRETT LARSON MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT is continued to February 28, 2024 per stipulation and order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ASIAN, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LASSEN HOUSING ASSOCIATES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Amended Notice Of Pending Action (Lis Pendens) And Request For Attorney'S Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 14, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT LASSEN HOUSING ASSOCIATES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA, BAYSIDE LASSEN, LLC., A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO EXPUNGE AMENDED NOTICE OF PENDING ACTION (LIS PENDENS) AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to establish probable validity of its claims. Attorney's fees granted as requested in the amount of $25,070. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
